irector , WirelessIOPort , WirelessToWired , WriteModel , Writer , XMLIcon , XMLInclusion , XMPPGateway , XMPPSink , XMPPSource , XSLTransformer , XYPlotter , XYScope , ZeroOrderHold public interface ModelErrorHandler Interface for model error handle

ModelErrorHandler Interface for model error handlers. A model error is an exception that is passed up the Ptolemy II hierarchy for handling until a container with a registered error handler is found. If there is no registered error handler, then

d. If there is no registered error handler, then the error is ignored. It is like throwing an exception, except that instead of unraveling the calling stack, it travels up the Ptolemy II hierarchy. A typical use of this facility is where an acto

y. A typical use of this facility is where an actor does the following: handleModelError(this, new IllegalActionException(this, message)); instead of this: throw new IllegalActionException(this, message); The former allows a container in the hierarch

ge); The former allows a container in the hierarchy to intercept the exception, whereas the latter simply throws the exception. Since: Ptolemy II 2.1 Version: $Id$ Author: Edward A. Lee Pt.AcceptedRating: Green (hyzheng) Pt.ProposedRating: Green (ea

ionException Handle a model error. Parameters: context - The object in which the error occurred. exception - An exception that represents the error. Returns: True if the error has been handled, or false if the error is not handled. Throws: Illegal
